    Start by exploring the AI Catalog in SAP Discovery Center. As explained in the first question, there you'll find the complete list of available agents, along with details about their capabilities, availability status (GA, Early Adopter Care, or Beta), and pricing.

    Step 2: Evaluate and Trial
    If you find an agent that fits your needs, check whether it's available for trial. The Discovery Center provides all the necessary information, including technical prerequisites such as BTP subaccount requirements, entitlements, connectivity settings, and configuration details.

    Step 3: Provision the Agent
    Once your organization has purchased the required entitlements, you can provision the agent directly through SAP for Me. We're also introducing enhanced capabilities that will guide you through the provisioning process, including the selection of prerequisite landscapes. BTP will handle the necessary connections in the background, as these are already configured within your BTP landscape- making activation smooth and seamless.

    What if I am still on-premise? Joule agents are cloud-native and run on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). On-premise customers can access these agents by connecting their systems to BTP via integration solutions such as SAP Integration Suite or BTP connectivity services. This allows them to leverage the power of AI agents while keeping their core business systems on-premise.

    Read on to get answers to your top questions from the session in this blog.

In this three-part tutorial series, you can rewatch how Joule Agents embedded in core business processes such as CX, HR, and Finance help organizations move from insight to action. You’ll learn where agents can proactively support decisions, execute process steps, and reduce manual effort.

Human Resource Agent: Shift into a manager's perspective. In this demo, we’ll focus on how managers not only support their teams day to day but also track and evaluate performance over time. A key part of this is the regular review cycles, whether quarterly, mid-year, or annual, where managers assess progress, provide feedback, and guide their teams toward better outcomes. You’ll see how this process can be streamlined and enhanced with intelligent support.

 

Customer Experience Agent: Step into the role of a sales representative or sales manager within the Sales Cloud application. You’ll see how users can easily access their assigned leads and opportunities, and more importantly, how they can take the next best actions supported by intelligent sales and service agents. The focus is on how AI-driven insights help prioritize work, guide decision-making, and ultimately drive more efficient and effective sales execution.

 

Finance Agent: Let’s put ourselves in the shoes of an accountant. In this demo, we’ll focus on one of the more time-consuming aspects of finance, managing accruals during month-end, quarter-end, or year-end cycles. While finance should enable strategic insights, much of the effort often goes into repetitive administrative tasks like journal entries and estimations. You’ll see how an accounting accrual agent can take on this workload, helping automate entries, improve accuracy, and free up time for more value-driven activities.

 

Want to dive deeper into the content? Rewatch the session and access the session material:

  • Accelerate your transformation to the Cloud with SAP Business AI (recording) 
  • Efficient Document Inbound Processing in SAP Cloud ERP with SAP Document AI (recording)

Hi Joule, where should I get started with the latest learning resources? *

Apart from live enablement sessions, explore our latest on-demand learning resources:

  • Introducing Joule Learning Journey: Understand Joule and the business value it provides, its core components, role‑based context‑aware workflows, and adoption essentials.

* Powered by the machine learning technology of SAP AI Business Services, personalized recommendations are generated for employees, in one place on the Learning home page, based on employee data.

As we look ahead, we’re excited to share with you a new lineup of learning opportunities designed to help you expand your knowledge and make AI an advantage in your role:

  • April 8, 15:00 CET: Agent Assisted Coding with AI Core. Explore how to leverage SAP AI Core's Generative AI Hub to enable agent-assisted coding workflows in your enterprise development environment.
  • April 9, 10 am CET: Build AI securely -Avoid Pitfalls in the Development and Operations Lifecycle. Learn more about common security pitfalls across development and operations, from secret leaks in version control to missing checks and misconfigured infrastructure that expose credentials.
  • April 14, 3 pm CET: Activating and Monitoring SAP Business AI in SAP for Me. Learn how to activate SAP Business AI capabilities and monitor AI consumption using SAP for Me.

Stay tuned for more, and happy learning! 🚀

Further Resources:

  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This October
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This November
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This December
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This January
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This February

Joule Agentic AI business process transformation Security

entry.title: Governing the Emerging Collective Intelligence
entry.content:
Artificial intelligence is often described as a technological breakthrough. But what if it is something more fundamental? Today’s AI systems are trained on vast amounts of human-generated data, documents, transactions, conversations, and decisions. In doing so, they do not simply process information. They reorganise fragments of our shared intellectual and cultural history.

When AI systems retrieve and interpret this data, they surface not only knowledge, but also ambiguity, bias, contradiction, and unresolved human tension. What emerges is not purely artificial intelligence-it is a reflection of humanity itself. This raises a profound question: Are we building systems that replicate human reasoning, or systems that externalise our collective mind?

From Collective Unconscious to Data Lakes

Carl Jung introduced the idea of the collective unconscious—a deeper layer of shared psychological structures that shape how humans perceive, think, and act. Modern enterprises are, in many ways, building a technological equivalent. Data lakes, enterprise platforms, and AI systems aggregate vast amounts of collective experience—sales interactions, customer behaviour, operational decisions—into shared repositories. From these, patterns emerge. Insights form. Decisions are guided.

But there is a critical difference. The collective unconscious was invisible. Enterprise intelligence systems are not. They are queryable, operational, and increasingly autonomous. This shift transforms something deeply human into something actionable—and scalable.

The Mirror and the Shadow

If AI systems reflect our collective knowledge, they also reflect our limitations. They can amplify:

  • Biases embedded in historical data
  • Misaligned incentives within organisations
  • Incomplete or conflicting interpretations of reality

In this sense, AI is not just a tool. It is a mirror—one that shows both clarity and distortion. Without proper governance, this mirror does not simply reflect reality. It can reshape it.

From Intelligence to Responsibility

This is where the conversation must evolve. AI governance is often framed as a technical or regulatory concern—focused on compliance, accuracy, and control. These are necessary, but not sufficient. If AI represents an externalised form of collective intelligence, then governance becomes something more: AI governance is the stewardship of how human knowledge, decisions, and meaning are captured, interpreted, and amplified by machines.

This introduces new responsibilities for organisations:

  • Not only to manage data, but to understand what it represents
  • Not only to deploy AI, but to question what it reinforces
  • Not only to optimise outcomes, but to ensure alignment with human intent

The Enterprise Imperative

For enterprises, this shift is already happening. AI copilots, assistants, and agents are becoming embedded in daily workflows—supporting sellers, guiding decisions, and interpreting complex systems like compensation, forecasting, and customer engagement. These systems do more than automate tasks. They shape how people understand reality within the organisation. And that makes governance not optional—but foundational.

From SaaS to Governing Intelligence: The Role of Joule

Enterprise software is undergoing a fundamental transformation. For decades, SaaS systems were designed to store, process, and expose data. Their primary role was to provide structure, consistency, and transactional reliability across business processes. With the rise of AI, this role is expanding. Systems are no longer just systems of record or systems of engagement. They are becoming systems of interpretation—capable of explaining outcomes, guiding decisions, and interacting with users in natural language. This is where SAP Joule represents a significant shift. Joule is not simply an AI assistant embedded into enterprise applications. It acts as an interface to enterprise intelligence, connecting data, processes, and business context into a unified layer of understanding. But more importantly, Joule introduces the foundation for something new: A governed layer of enterprise intelligence.

As AI agents begin to interpret sensitive domains such as compensation, performance, customer interactions, and financial outcomes, the need for trusted interpretation becomes critical. This moves SAP’s role beyond delivering SaaS applications. It positions SAP as a provider of:

  • Context-aware intelligence, grounded in enterprise data
  • Traceable reasoning, connecting outputs back to underlying transactions
  • Governed AI interactions, aligned with business rules, policies, and intent

In this model, Joule is not only assisting users—it is operating within a controlled and explainable framework of enterprise knowledge. This is the transition:

  • From delivering applications
  • To enabling decisions
  • To govern how intelligence itself is produced and consumed within the enterprise

Why This Matters-As organisations adopt AI at scale, the risk is not only incorrect answers, but ungoverned meaning.

Without a governing layer, AI may generate plausible but ungrounded interpretations. Decisions may drift from policy and intent. Trust in enterprise systems may erode. With Joule and SAP’s AI foundation, there is an opportunity to ensure that:

  • Intelligence is grounded in enterprise truth
  • Outputs are explainable and auditable
  • AI becomes a trusted extension of the organisation, not an uncontrolled external layer

A Strategic Shift-This marks a broader transformation: From Software as a Service → to Governed Intelligence as a Capability

In this future, the value of enterprise platforms will not be defined only by the data they store—but by how responsibly they interpret and operationalise that data through AI.

A New Discipline

We are entering a new phase of AI adoption—one where the question is no longer what AI can do, but what it should represent. This requires a new discipline that connects:

  • Technology and psychology
  • Data and meaning
  • Automation and accountability

Because in the end, the systems we build will not only reflect our intelligence—They will reflect our values.

As enterprises continue to build and deploy AI, one question should remain at the centre: If AI becomes the voice of our collective intelligence—who ensures it speaks with clarity, responsibility, and truth?
